Details:
In one study, removal using ozone was near 65% with only a few seconds of contact time, suggesting simazine may be sufficiently ozone reactive to be cost effective [1431].  In another study, atrazine was well controlled in a river water at pHs 6 and 9 in 30 minutes at ozone doses up to 5 mg/L, suggesting structurally similar simazine may be likely reactive with ozone [1428].
Deethylsimazine was not reactive with ozone in at pHs 6 and 9 in 30 minutes at ozone doses up to 5 mg/L [1428].
